response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong testing discipline, distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (low-latency, high-throughput services), and genuine geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as primary languages, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ work at National Instruments a decade ago and no Rust. Recent work is TypeScript/Node-heavy. Geospatial and low-latency backgrounds are relevant, but the core language gap is significant for a Senior IC role centered on C++ routing tiling.
strengths: Geospatial domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map, GIS web apps)
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems background at Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship
concerns: No demonstrated modern C++ experience; C/C++ only at NI in 2014–2015
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node, not systems-level languages
